It sounds like you're managing multiple complex areas of your life at once—professionally, personally, and even dealing with residual concerns from past health experiences. When a past event, such as your surgical experience during early adulthood, resurfaces and triggers anxiety, it can make balancing your current responsibilities even more challenging. One approach that might help is developing a routine that includes regular self-care practices, such as mindfulness or relaxation techniques, which can help ease health-related anxiety when those memories arise. Additionally, setting clear boundaries between work and personal time can allow you space to process these emotions without overwhelming your day. It might also be beneficial to talk with a professional who can help you explore these feelings and perhaps reframe the way you view past events, so they no longer hinder your progress. Remember, acknowledging these memories is a brave first step toward holistic self-care, and small adjustments in your routine can gradually build a stronger foundation for both your personal well-being and your professional pursuits.
